page: Microsoft Azure DC48eds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the DCEDV6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 Intel processor architecture. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core.

This server configuration includes 2835 GB of local storage and is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk subsystems. It does not feature any integrated graphics processing units and includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the DC48eds_v6 provides a balanced allocation of dedicated compute cores and high-capacity local storage, making it suitable for enterprise workloads that require consistent CPU performance. Appropriate workloads include memory-intensive applications, relational databases, and data warehousing tasks that benefit from local high-speed storage and dedicated virtualization resources.
description: Microsoft Azure DC48eds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the DCEDV6 family, powered by an Intel x86_64 processor and running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For storage-heavy workloads, the instance includes 2835 GB of local disk space and supports Premium Storage configurations. This server does not include any GPU resources or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The hardware profile is designed for enterprise-grade workloads requiring dedicated compute resources and substantial local storage capacity, such as relational databases, memory-intensive business applications, and data processing pipelines.
